name: Sendero del Río Verde en Otívar – Barranco de las Chortales
region_intro: Hiking around Otívar offers diverse terrain within the province of Granada, Spain, situated at the foot of the Sierra de Cazulas and Sierra de Almijara mountains. The region is characterized by rugged hillsides, steep slopes, and the prominent Río Verde valley, known for its clear, emerald-green waters and ravines. This area benefits from a subtropical microclimate, supporting lush vegetation and providing a unique blend of mountain and river environments. Best hiking trails around Otívar The most popular hiking route is Sendero del Río Verde en Otívar – Barranco de las Chortales, a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate path leads through the Río Verde valley, featuring waterfalls and natural pools. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of the Sierra de Almijara – Cueva de Funes Reservoir loop from Poyos del Pescado, a difficult 6.0 miles (9.7 km) path. This route offers expansive views of the Sierra de Almijara and passes by the Cueva de Funes Reservoir. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Troncos Petrificados, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 25 minutes. Hiking around Otívar is defined by dramatic mountain ranges, the vibrant Río Verde valley, and subtropical plantations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging mountain routes. The routes in Otívar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 1,000 reviews. More than 4,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Otívar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Otívar?
A: Otívar offers a selection of around 17 hiking routes, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These trails are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.2 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Otívar?
A: The terrain around Otívar is quite diverse, ranging from rugged hillsides and steep slopes in the Sierra de Cazulas and Sierra de Almijara mountains to the lush, subtropical environment of the Río Verde valley. You'll find paths winding through gorges, alongside rivers, and offering panoramic mountain views.

Q: Are there any trails with waterfalls or opportunities for swimming?
A: Absolutely! The Río Verde valley is famous for its crystal-clear, emerald-green waters, numerous waterfalls, and natural pools, making it ideal for refreshing swims. A popular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e978098870/sendero-del-rio-verde-en-otivar-barranco-de-las-chortales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Río Verde en Otívar – Barranco de las Chortales&lt;/a&gt;, which leads directly to spectacular waterfalls and pools. You can also explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/87627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Verde Waterfalls, Barranco de las Chortales&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/381716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Higuerón River Gorge and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Otívar?
A: Yes, Otívar offers easier trails suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38260371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Cázulas&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.7-mile (4.3 km) route that explores varied landscapes and can be completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes. The Molineta Walk, starting near the municipal swimming pool, is another less strenuous option with beautiful scenery.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I discover while hiking?
A: Hikers can enjoy breathtaking vistas of the surrounding mountains, including the Sierras of Alhama, Tejeda, and Almijara, as well as the Río Verde Valley. Notable highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/671582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo de los Bueyes Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/818164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Lucero (1,779 m)&lt;/a&gt; summit. The Molineta Walk also offers a bird's-eye view of Otívar.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Otívar?
A: Otívar benefits from a unique subtropical microclimate, which ensures pleasant conditions for outdoor activities for much of the year. This makes it an excellent destination for hiking during spring, autumn, and even mild winter days, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38260371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Cázulas&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy option. For a more challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19343378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Almijara – Cueva de Funes Reservoir loop from Poyos del Pescado&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive views.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Otívar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 1,000 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural beauty of the Río Verde valley, the diverse landscapes, and the opportunities for refreshing dips in natural pools.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Beyond natural beauty, some trails may lead you past historical elements such as the Arabic palace or the Palacete de Cázulas, adding a cultural dimension to your outdoor adventures. The area around Cázulas, for example, has historical significance.

Q: What makes the Río Verde valley special for hikers?
A: The Río Verde valley is a major draw due to its crystal-clear, emerald-green waters, numerous waterfalls, and deep ravines. It offers a unique blend of hiking and aquatic activities, with opportunities for refreshing swims in turquoise pools and even canyoning in some sections. The valley's lush subtropical vegetation also adds to its distinct charm.

Q: Are there any easy hikes for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk?
A: Certainly. For an easy and enjoyable walk, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38260371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Cázulas&lt;/a&gt; is a great choice, taking about 1 hour 21 minutes. The Molineta Walk is another gentle uphill path offering amazing scenery without being overly strenuous.

Q: Where can I find challenging hikes with expansive views?
A: For more challenging routes with breathtaking vistas, consider trails that ascend into the Sierra de Almijara. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19343378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Almijara – Cueva de Funes Reservoir loop from Poyos del Pescado&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 7.0-mile (11.3 km) path that provides expansive views of the Sierra de Almijara and passes by the Cueva de Funes Reservoir.
tips: Spectacular geological formation. The erosion caused by the passage of water has left large grooves in the limestone rock.
Very nice shady place to stay. You can even swim. Very refreshing, especially on hot summer days.
Pool formed by a large waterfall in the Verde River, perfect for bathing although sometimes it can be quite crowded.
